libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: When tests exercise Guice method interception, CGLIB proxy creation, or other runtime bytecode generation, configure the test JVM and native-image build with `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` as the existing 2.1.7 test scaffold does. Prefer public Guice APIs (`com.google.inject.*`, `com.google.inject.matcher.Matchers`, `org.aopalliance.intercept.*`) and avoid importing the old unprefixed internal classes from the 2.1.7 tests; in 2.9.1 many shaded internal classes are present under `$`-prefixed names and are not stable user-facing APIs.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The reported javac failure is caused by tests importing internal classes whose names changed between 2.1.7 and 2.9.1, not by a missing external dependency.
Basic injector/binding coverage can run without extra setup, but AOP/proxy coverage fails on strongly encapsulated JDKs without the add-opens JVM/native-image argument.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker image is needed; the resolved artifact carries Guice core plus shaded CGLIB/ASM internals and declares javax.inject/aopalliance dependencies, but meaningful AOP/proxy coverage on modern JDKs needs java.lang opened for old CGLIB reflective class definition.
